item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
you have made me sympathize with an entity created by a thought experiment I always kinda hated.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was going through the available numbers and my standard 4*05 are all used up so I searched for alternatives. I did the "….nice" when I saw a *69 was available! And here we are.

Thank you for your feedback, I appreciate your help a lot in toning this article up.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Read it, Loved it, Critted it, and it just got better.

Poor 48691 though, because that's one screwed up way to be born and die, having lived a life where you thought you had a family but really you were just a brain the whole time.

Also that ending was perfect.

Footnotes
1. nice

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Hello friends and welcome to my 12th mainlist SCP article. (I think?)

I have long been inspired by whacky thought exercises and in this piece, I drew inspiration from one such experiment called a Boltzmann Brain. It's an exercise in conceptual absurdity somewhat similar to Schroedinger's Cat. While I enjoy the underlying theories and discussing them, I am nowhere near smart enough to thoroughly and accurately explain these to you so in the interests of not doing damage, I'd encourage you to educate yourself.

I'd like a moment to give special recognition to Henzoid, T Rutherford, and SCPD Discord Member "Lilly" for their help in providing extended feedback for this piece.

The initial draft was created under the constraints of the Bright challenge.
